# Immune-system local/cloud scoping: gate B1 docker-socket sibling-restart to cloud; keep bridge + data-integrity + B0 local

## Context (V-B-A'd 2026-06-26)

The orchestrator scopes tasks by `deploymentMode` (`config.mjs:389`, defaults `'local'`) into three buckets: `localOnly{}` (local-on/cloud-off), `cloudOnly{}` (cloud-on/local-off, e.g. `tenant-repo-sync`), and "both" (ungated). See `Orchestrator.mjs:61-90` (`resolveDeploymentEnabled` / `resolveCloudDeploymentEnabled`).

**The 4 deployment-immune-system services â€” `DeploymentStateBridge`, `ContainerHealthDiagnosis`, `DataIntegrityDiagnosis`, `RecoveryActuator` â€” are in NEITHER bucket.** They run local+cloud by **omission, not a deliberate decision**.

## The decision (operator-confirmed 2026-06-26)

Split by what each half actually does:
- **Keep LOCAL (genuinely "both"):** the read-only `DeploymentStateBridge` snapshot (feeds the agent-facing `get_deployment_state_snapshot` / `inspect_deployment` tools â€” real local value); the **data-integrity** detect + in-process re-embed heal (local memory-core/chroma rot like cloud; in-process, no socket); the **B0** in-process child recycle (the stuck-local-model-server class, #13900).
- **Gate to CLOUD:** the **B1 docker-socket sibling-container restart**. ADR-25's motivating case is "a sibling saturates *and the human-repair path is unavailable*" â€” the no-operator cloud scenario. Locally the operator is present and autonomously recycling a dev container is disruptive. B1 is the privileged tier (ADR-26 Â§2.2) and is likely dormant locally anyway (the socket resolves known compose labels a local non-compose run may lack), so gating it is low-risk hygiene that makes the implicit classification explicit.

## Coordination

Interacts with ADR-26 Â§2.2 activation default (#13952: recovery actuator enabled-by-default, opt-out blocklists). This refines that for the B1 tier under `deploymentMode==='local'`. **@neo-opus-grace owns the recovery actuator** â€” coordinate before implementing.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] B1 docker-socket sibling-container restart is gated off when `deploymentMode==='local'` (mirror the `cloudOnly` pattern, or an equivalent deploymentMode gate on the B1 path).
- [ ] Bridge + data-integrity detect/re-embed + B0 in-process recycle remain enabled in local mode.
- [ ] The classification is explicit (deliberate local/cloud/both), not "by omission".
- [ ] ADR-25/26 updated if the gating changes the documented activation default.

Refs ADR-0025, ADR-0026, #13952 (activation default), #14039 (epic), #14147 (sibling). Operator-confirmed direction. Authored by Ada (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

## Timeline

- 2026-06-26T22:00:30Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #14152
- 2026-06-26T22:04:02Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #14148

- 2026-06-27T00:08:26Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #14175
- 2026-06-27T00:17:25Z @tobiu referenced in commit `2c3c401` - "feat(ai): cloudOnly composeServiceRecoveryEnabled mode-gate for B1 docker-socket recovery (#14150) (#14170)

The 4 immune-system services ran 'both by omission' (neither localOnly nor cloudOnly). The B1 docker-socket sibling-container restart is cloud-oriented (ADR-25's no-operator-cloud scenario); locally the operator is present + autonomously recycling a dev container is disruptive. Adds a cloudOnly.composeServiceRecoveryEnabled leaf + the resolveCloudOnlyEnabled getter (mirroring tenantRepoSyncEnabled) â€” cloud defaults enabled, local disabled, operator-overridable. Exports resolveCloudOnlyEnabled so the recovery actuator's B1-selection point can consult it cross-module (ADR-0019-clean, no scattered deploymentMode reads). Orthogonal to blockedComposeServices (mode-gate vs per-service opt-out).

Per @neo-opus-grace's #14150 plan: I own the stable surface (leaf + getter + export + tests); she wires the one-line consult at the B1-selection point in her #14134 cutover. B0 in-process recycle + data-integrity re-embed + the read-only bridge stay local. 18/18 specs incl. config-template-ssot lint.
